channel.title: NPR Programs: All Things Considered
channel.description: All Things Considered hosts Ailsa Chang, Mary Louise Kelly, Juana Summers and Scott Detrow present the program's trademark mix of news, interviews, commentaries, reviews, and offbeat features 7 days a week.
channel.copyright: Copyright 2024 NPR - For Personal Use Only

item.title: Homesick in a foreign country, a teenager meets a lifelong friend
item.description: Holly Deiaco-Smith was feeling homesick while studying abroad in France when she was 19 years old. An encounter at the post office changed everything and led to a decades-long friendship.
item.content:encoded: Holly Deiaco-Smith was feeling homesick while studying abroad in France when she was 19 years old. An encounter at the post office changed everything and led to a decades-long friendship.
item.dc:creator: Autumn Barnes

item.title: Who are the plaintiffs in landmark birthright citizenship case?
item.description: The Supreme Court hears arguments in the Trump administration's bid to deny birthright citizenship to the US-born children of undocumented immigrants. Who are the plaintiffs in Trump v. Barbara.
item.content:encoded: The Supreme Court hears arguments in the Trump administration's bid to deny birthright citizenship to the US-born children of undocumented immigrants. Who are the plaintiffs in Trump v. Barbara.
item.dc:creator: Adrian Florido

item.title: Here to help: These volunteers build and maintain hiking trails in North Carolina
item.description: When you hike or ride a bike in Charlotte, N.C., it's likely you're on a path built and maintained by volunteers. We'll meet them in our series Here to Help.
item.content:encoded: When you hike or ride a bike in Charlotte, N.C., it's likely you're on a path built and maintained by volunteers. We'll meet them in our series Here to Help.
item.dc:creator: Eric Teel

item.title: Kherson, Ukraine: A hometown changed (Pt. 1)
item.description: Four years of Russia's all-out war on Ukraine have transformed not only Ukrainian cities but also how modern warfare is waged, in the first of this two-part story from Kherson.
item.content:encoded: Four years of Russia's all-out war on Ukraine have transformed not only Ukrainian cities but also how modern warfare is waged, in the first of this two-part story from Kherson.
item.dc:creator: Joanna Kakissis

item.title: Taiwan reacts to U.S. intelligence report about China's intentions
item.description: A recent U.S. intelligence report reignited an already contentious debate in Taiwan over China's intentions and how Taipei should reasonably defend itself against its powerful neighbor.
item.content:encoded: A recent U.S. intelligence report reignited an already contentious debate in Taiwan over China's intentions and how Taipei should reasonably defend itself against its powerful neighbor.
item.dc:creator: Ashish Valentine

item.title: Iranians debate whether the war is worth it
item.description: As the war in Iran enters its second month, and President Trump signals an end to the war, many Iranians are urging the U.S and Israel to keep striking their country.
item.content:encoded:
As the war in Iran enters its second month, and President Trump signals an end to the war, many Iranians are urging the U.S and Israel to keep striking their country.

(Image credit: Atta Kenare)
item.dc:creator: Emily Feng

item.title: The Supreme Court struck down a ban on conversion therapy in Colorado
item.description: A Colorado law banning talk therapy that seeks to change a teenager's sexual orientation or gender identity has been rejected by the Supreme Court. LGBTQ advocates are not happy.
item.content:encoded: A Colorado law banning talk therapy that seeks to change a teenager's sexual orientation or gender identity has been rejected by the Supreme Court. LGBTQ advocates are not happy.
item.dc:creator: Nina Totenberg

item.title: In Pentagon briefings, Hegseth leans into religious rhetoric
item.description: Justification for starting a war with Iran have been inconsistent and sometimes contradictory from U.S. officials, but the language has also been different than in wars past.
item.content:encoded: Justification for starting a war with Iran have been inconsistent and sometimes contradictory from U.S. officials, but the language has also been different than in wars past.
item.dc:creator: Odette Yousef

item.title: A foreign policy adviser to Netanyahu talks about Israel's stance on war with Iran
item.description: NPR's Juana Summers speaks with Ophir Falk, foreign policy adviser to Israeli Prime Minister Benjamin Netanyahu, about his country's stance on war with Iran and Hezbollah.
item.content:encoded: NPR's Juana Summers speaks with Ophir Falk, foreign policy adviser to Israeli Prime Minister Benjamin Netanyahu, about his country's stance on war with Iran and Hezbollah.
item.dc:creator: Juana Summers

item.title: NASA once touted the diversity of Artemis II's astronauts. Now? Not so much
item.description: The Artemis II mission crew contains four people -- including one woman and one Black man, both of whom will be the first on a lunar mission. But NASA hasn't been talking about these milestones much.
item.content:encoded: The Artemis II mission crew contains four people -- including one woman and one Black man, both of whom will be the first on a lunar mission. But NASA hasn't been talking about these milestones much.
item.dc:creator: Katia Riddle
